Feature.Description: Turn the DENSO robot arm to face forward or reverse by running a dedicated turn PacScript over b-CAP. "forward" brings the arm to the retract pose; "reverse" brings it to the 180°-turned inverse retract pose. The command may only be called while the arm is at one of the four known poses (base, retract, inverse base, inverse retract), so the turn starts from a safe, known posture.

Command.Description: Turn the arm to the requested orientation. Direction "forward" runs the forward turn task and ends at the retract pose; "reverse" runs the reverse turn task and ends at the inverse retract pose. Requires the arm to start at one of the four known poses. Intermediate responses report the current phase.

Response.Description: The orientation reached: "forward" (retract pose) or "reverse" (inverse retract pose).

IntermediateResponse.Description: The current phase of the turn sequence.

DefinedExecutionError.Description: The Direction is not one of "forward" or "reverse".
The arm is not at any of the four known poses (base, retract, inverse base, inverse retract); the turn is refused.
The arm did not end at the expected pose (retract for forward, inverse retract for reverse) after the turn task.
Failed to connect to or communicate with the robot controller over b-CAP.
Failed to acquire the robot or read the current joint angles.
The turn task (PAC program) did not start or finish within the configured timeout.
The turn task failed to run or stopped abnormally.
